q1.scenario: A free weekend opens up. You'd rather—

options.a: Go somewhere on a whim
options.b: Finish what you'd planned at home

q2.scenario: Facing a big decision, you usually—

options.a: Listen to your gut first
options.b: Lay out the pros and cons first

q3.scenario: You walk into a party full of strangers. You'll—

options.a: Work the room and become the center of it
options.b: Find one person you click with and go deep

q4.scenario: A conflict flares up. Your first move is to—

options.a: Hash it out face to face, right now
options.b: Step back and process it on your own first

q5.scenario: Handed a brand-new project, you tend to—

options.a: Just start, and figure it out as you go
options.b: Map the plan and the steps first

options.a: Move your body, or do something competitive
options.b: Read and quietly learn something

q7.scenario: A friend vents a problem to you. You'd rather—

options.a: Help them analyze it and offer fixes
options.b: Just listen and feel it with them

options.a: Tidy and ordered, everything in its place
options.b: Spread out and free, inspiration within reach

options.a: Build a detailed itinerary
options.b: Wander, no plan at all

q10.scenario: When a strong feeling hits, you tend to—

options.a: Show it, let it be seen
options.b: Hold it in and handle it quietly

q11.scenario: On a team, the role you fall into is—

options.a: The one who sets direction and calls it
options.b: The one who fills gaps and nails the details

q12.scenario: Faced with a decent status quo, you'd rather—

options.a: Chase the new idea that just popped up
options.b: Polish what already works into something better

q13.scenario: A risky opportunity is on the table. You'll—

options.a: Bet on the upside
options.b: Protect what you already have first

q14.scenario: Challenged to your face, you're more likely to—

options.a: Argue your case and make the point
options.b: Go quiet, take it in, revisit later

q15.scenario: With a deadline ahead, you usually—

options.a: Move early and steady, leaving slack
options.b: Push it to the end and sprint

q16.scenario: After back-to-back new faces, you feel—

options.a: More alive the more you talk—fully charged
options.b: A bit spent, needing alone time to recharge

q17.scenario: Toward the rules as given, you're closer to—

options.a: Question them, and bend them when needed
options.b: Respect the structure—the rules feel safer

options.a: The beauty in a piece of art, a feeling
options.b: Something genuinely useful that solves a problem

q19.scenario: Joining a group effort to help, you'd rather—

options.a: Organize it and split the work
options.b: Tend to people's feelings and needs

q20.scenario: If one line had to capture you—

options.a: I act
options.b: I build
